title: Executive reporting pipeline recovery
business_context: A fictional executive reporting cycle is approaching while freshness checks show delayed data and incomplete partitions.
initial_stakes: Executive metrics are stale, downstream decisions may use incomplete data, and a controlled recovery is required.

turns.situation: Freshness alerts show a four-hour lag and one source partition has stopped advancing.

options.label: Correlate offsets, quality checks, and dependency health
options.description: Identify the failed boundary before changing pipeline state.
options.expected_outcome: The failed partition and affected reports are isolated.

options.label: Start a full historical backfill
options.description: Reprocess all data before isolating the fault.
options.expected_outcome: Compute load and recovery uncertainty increase.

turns.situation: The failed partition is identified and downstream tables contain mixed freshness.

options.label: Freeze report publication and validate source state
options.description: Prevent incomplete metrics from reaching decision makers.
options.expected_outcome: Reporting confidence is protected during recovery.

options.label: Publish available partial metrics
options.description: Release mixed-freshness reports without confidence labels.
options.expected_outcome: Decision makers may act on inconsistent metrics.

turns.situation: The recovery owner needs approval to replay the isolated partition.

options.label: Escalate recovery plan with validation checkpoints
options.description: Assign owners for replay, quality, and stakeholder updates.
options.expected_outcome: Recovery proceeds with accountable quality gates.

options.label: Replay the partition without a checkpoint
options.description: Start recovery without named quality ownership.
options.expected_outcome: Data moves, but release confidence remains uncertain.

turns.situation: The isolated replay completes and quality checks must confirm downstream consistency.

options.label: Compare row counts, freshness, and reconciliation controls
options.description: Validate recovery across source and reporting layers.
options.expected_outcome: The team confirms that reporting data is internally consistent.

options.label: Accept the first successful pipeline run
options.expected_outcome: Undetected quality defects may reach executive reports.

turns.situation: Quality gates pass and the reporting service can return to normal publication.

options.label: Restore reporting gradually with confidence notes
options.description: Release validated reports and monitor freshness checkpoints.
options.expected_outcome: Reporting resumes with transparent confidence and monitoring.

options.label: Publish every delayed report at once
options.description: Release all outputs without a monitored transition.
options.expected_outcome: A second quality issue would affect all reporting consumers.
